name: Picón to Porzuna – Circular from
name_original: Picón a Porzuna – circular desde Piedrabuena
description: This difficult 40.0-mile mountain biking route from Piedrabuena to Picón and Porzuna offers a significant physical challenge.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Piedrabuena offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the hard quartzite hills of the Sierra de Picón and unique volcanic elements. The region features explosion craters that house lagoons, adding a distinctive geological character to the terrain. The Bullaque River also flows through the area, creating important riparian habitats. This combination of varied topography provides a compelling setting for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails in Piedrabuena The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadiana Natural Way and El Chiquero Loop, a 41.9 miles (67.4 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 44 minutes to complete, offering a significant physical test through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Picón to Porzuna – Circular from, a difficult 39.9 miles (64.3 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain, traversing the Sierra de Picón with its characteristic "Appalachian-like" relief. Local mountain bikers also love the Campground Hostel – Bridge over the Bullaque River loop from Los Rubiales, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail leading through the Bullaque River area, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Piedrabuena is defined by the hard quartzite hills of the Sierra de Picón, unique volcanic craters, and the ecological richness of the Bullaque River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Piedrabuena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Piedrabuena?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Piedrabuena, offering a variety of experiences. These include 5 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Piedrabuena?
A: The terrain around Piedrabuena is diverse, characterized by the hard quartzite hills of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Picón&lt;/strong&gt;, which exhibit an 'Appalachian-like' relief. You'll also encounter unique volcanic elements, including explosion craters that house lagoons, and the riparian habitats along the &lt;strong&gt;Bullaque River&lt;/strong&gt;. Expect varying elevations and landscapes covered with dense scrubland.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find challenging long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041124199/guadiana-natural-way-and-el-chiquero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Way and El Chiquero Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.9-mile (67.4 km) trail. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026524880/picon-to-porzuna-circular-from" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón to Porzuna – Circular from&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 39.9 miles (64.3 km) with substantial elevation gain through the Sierra de Picón.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Mountain biking in Piedrabuena offers views of several notable features. You can pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3497307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;observation tower&lt;/a&gt;, or spot &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3728650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;stork nests&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique volcanic lagoons like Lucianego, and the ecological richness of the Bullaque River area.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: Absolutely. A popular moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campground Hostel – Bridge over the Bullaque River loop from Los Rubiales&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.8 miles (59.2 km) long and takes about 4 hours 43 minutes. This trail leads through the scenic Bullaque River area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Piedrabuena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the natural beauty of the Sierra de Picón and Bullaque River areas.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Piedrabuena?
A: Yes, many routes in Piedrabuena are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026524880/picon-to-porzuna-circular-from" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón to Porzuna – Circular from&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10349114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campground Hostel – CR-4171 Bridge loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The riparian habitats along the Bullaque River are particularly rich in wildlife. You might spot otters and various reptiles, including the European pond turtle. The river also supports native fish species such as boga, calandino, and comizo barbel.

Q: Are there any facilities or beaches near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;, you can find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Beaches&lt;/a&gt;, which offer recreational opportunities and facilities. The area around the dam is a popular spot for views and relaxation.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Several routes offer views of or pass by lakes and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, and you might also encounter other lagoons, including those formed in volcanic explosion craters, such as Lucianego or La Laguna near Piedrabuena.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Piedrabuena?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes of Piedrabuena, with its scrubland and riverine areas, suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter.
tips: Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

It is in the Romanesque style and consists of a central nave crossed by an arm. In its later part stands the tower, of previous construction. It has valuable images, carvings and frescoes. Among the most important are the Christ of the Agony, a carving from the 17th century, and the Holy Cross, of Byzantine origin and carved in silver. In the same square is the Town Hall.

Porzuna was a Roman and Visigoth settlement, and it is from these civilizations that historical remains have been found. The first settlers of the region are probably from the Lower and Middle Paleolithic according to the archaeological remains found. Most of these can be found in the Ciudad Real museum.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Picón a Porzuna – circular desde Piedrabuena mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride on Komoot, requiring a very good fitness level. While Komoot also mentions it's suitable for all skill levels, the 'Hard' rating suggests advanced riding skills may be necessary, and some sections could even require pushing your bike. It's best suited for experienced riders looking for a significant challenge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 64.3 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 809 meters. It is estimated to take around 5 hours and 39 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Specific seasonal details for this route are not available, but generally, for mountain biking in this region, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring rain, making the trail less enjoyable or more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several religious buildings. You'll encounter the Our Lady of the Assumption in Piedrabuena, the Parish of El Salvador in Picón, and the Parish Church of Porzuna.

faqs.question: Is the Picón a Porzuna – circular desde Piedrabuena route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this particular route, it's a long and challenging mountain bike trail. If you plan to bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for the distance and terrain, and always keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any fees to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride this specific mountain bike route. However, it's always a good practice to respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Picón a Porzuna – circular desde Piedrabuena route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Piedrabuena. While specific parking facilities for this exact trailhead aren't detailed, you should be able to find public parking within Piedrabuena, particularly near the town center or residential areas, from where you can easily access the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Specific detailed descriptions of the terrain for this exact route are limited. However, given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation changes, expect varied terrain that could include unpaved roads, gravel paths, and potentially technical singletrack sections. The region often features winding paths through natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will ride along parts of the Cañada Real Segoviana, Porzuna - Alcolea de Calatrava, Alcolea de Calatrava - Los Pozuelos de Calatrava, Los Pozuelos de Calatrava - La Viñuela, and various segments of the Cañada Real Toledana, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Piedrabuena, and passes through Picón and Porzuna. These towns should offer basic amenities such as cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation options. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially if you plan to stay overnight or need specific services.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the Komoot app is highly recommended for navigation.
